description: Select an optimal k-stock portfolio from DOW components to balance return and risk, comparing QAOA-based quantum selection against classical Markowitz mean-variance optimization and brute-force enumeration.

inputs.data_source: DOW component stocks (AAPL, BA, CAT, CSCO, HD, IBM, JNJ, JPM, KO, MMM, MSFT, WMT), 2018-2022 OHLCV CSV
inputs.qubit_encoding: 1 qubit per stock; x_i = stock i selected

quantum_method: QAOA (p=2 layers, 5 restarts, COBYLA) with cqlib StatevectorSimulator; portfolio selection as QUBO
classical_baseline: Markowitz mean-variance optimization (continuous weights) + brute-force binary enumeration (exact for small n)
success_signals: QAOA finds optimal selection (gap 0%) on small-to-medium tiers (5-8 stocks)
QAOA scales to 12 stocks (full tier) producing a feasible portfolio
QAOA Sharpe ratio comparable to classical on small tiers
